47.reason: [The input reading logic is flawed: after processing an operation, it reads the next operation inside the if/else blocks, but if the last operation is -1, it may not be read correctly. Also, the loop condition checks n!=-1, but the initial read is outside the loop and subsequent reads happen only inside branches, which can miss reading the next operation after a pop or push if input ends unexpectedly. More critically, this structure fails to read the next operation after the last valid one before -1, potentially causing infinite loop or missed -1. The reference program uses a comma operator in while condition to read and check in one step, which is safer.]
